author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
A Treatise on the Gift of Perseverance.
It is God’s Grace Both that Man Comes to Him, and that Man Does Not Depart from Him.
originalBook: A Treatise on the Gift of Perseverance.
title: It is God’s Grace Both that Man Comes to Him, and that Man Does Not Depart from Him.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: This grace He placed “in Him in whom we have obtained a lot, being predestinated according to the purpose of Him who worketh all things.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: And thus as He worketh that we come to Him, so He worketh that we do not depart.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Wherefore it was said to Him by the mouth of the prophet, “Let Thy hand be upon the man of Thy right hand, and upon the Son of man whom Thou madest strong for Thyself, and we will not depart from Thee.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: This certainly is not the first Adam, in whom we departed from Him, but the second Adam, upon whom His hand is placed, so that we do not depart from Him.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For Christ altogether with His members is—for the Church’s sake, which is His body—the fulness of Him.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: When, therefore, God’s hand is upon Him, that we depart not from God, assuredly God’s work reaches to us (for this is God’s hand); by which work of God we are caused to be abiding in Christ with God—not, as in Adam, departing from God.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For “in Christ we have obtained a lot, being predestinated according to His purpose who worketh all things.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: This, therefore, is God’s hand, not ours, that we depart not from God.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: That, I say, is His hand who said, “I will put my fear in their hearts, that they depart not from me.”
